rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIE BEATRICE JACKSON-HENRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/8/26. (No earlier availability.) Examiner needs more time to review the petition online, as no courtesy copy was provided. No proof of service of notice has been filed. The petition does not appear to address whether or not the decedent was a surviving spouse of a person who received benefits reportable to the DHCS. It is unclear if all cash is being held in just one checking account at one "Bank". The petition does not disclose what happened to the securities. Paragraph 21 does not, in fact, indicate the addresses of the persons. The case number for the related forcible detainer action does not appear to have been set forth. It does not appear that the fees requested for extraordinary services were adequately supported, as required by CRC 7.703 and, if time was billed by a paralegal, the fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications regarding the requirements of, and compliance with, Business & Professions Code Section 6450. Deadline to provide courtesy copies of the petition and any new filed papers to the assigned examiner is 5/26/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUEY-JUN WONG TONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/8/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The caption of the pleading shown on the Notice of Hearing at item 1 was incorrect. Notice of the correct petition must be given for the 6/8/26 hearing. (2) If the real property was sold in 2025 (no date was set forth or any other information about the sale), why does the estate need to remain open for another year just to pay taxes from the sale? (3) How much in taxes is anticipated? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/26/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID LLEWELLYN BEVERLEY TROTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/03/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) The amendment to the petition filed 03/25/2026 requests statutory compensation of $25,600.46, although the underlying petition calculated the statutory compensation as $30,009.60. The amended does not provide the basis for computation. In a verified supplement, please clarify the appropriate amount of statutory compensation. 2) A petition for final distribution must include the complete legal description and APN of real property to be distributed (CRC 7.651(b)(3)). 3) The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed to each distributee, including the number of shares of securities and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) to be distributed to each beneficiary (LRSF 14.35.I.6.a). Please indicate the amount of the residue to be distributed to Parisa Kuhzarani. Please address the above issues and delivery courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 05/20/2026 to avoid additional conintuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO CANALES BECERRA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Spousal Property Petition
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues have not been cleared: (1) Petitioner alleges that her 2014 Transfer Trust severed the joint tenancy. The petition is silent on whether the transaction was made with the spouse's written consent during the marriage. (2) If the transfer was valid, what legal authority allows the Court to confirm a one-half interest in real property that was already given to the surviving spouse in 2014? It remains unclear how the Court could claim jurisdiction over property now held by a trust, not by the surviving spouse as an individual. (3) Petition to set forth all known heirs of the decedent Prob. Code sec 13651(a)(5). (4) Notice of hearing must be given to those heirs. Prob. Code sec. 13655(a)(2). Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance and Access must be given. LRSF 14.1.C. (5) Petition #3 to set forth the date of death. (6) The Court instructed counsel to provide supplemental information as requested by the title company and continued the petition to the current date. However, no additional information has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LIVING TRUST OF CERALINA HAFALIA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate First Amendment To The Trust; Financial Elder Abuse; To Enjoin Successor Trustee From Using Trust Funds To Fund Litigation; For
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. A copy of the proposed order must be served on each person entitled to notice in a contested matter. LRSF 14.7(A)(4). Proof of service must be filed separately to show compliance. 2) No mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120 was used to give timely (30-day) notice of this petition with a copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) to Renaldo Hafalia who has not made a general appearance unlike respondent. Petitioner used a DE-115 form to give notice without attaching the remote appearance information, but the petition does not seek relief under Probate Code section 850 (for which the DE-115 form would have been the mandatory Judicial Council form), and item 3 of the DE-115 form did not list any property sought to be recovered in any event (see section 851(c)(1)). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM T. COX
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether the effort to locate decedent's heirs-at-law is sufficient (San Franciso Local Rule 14.18.C.1). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LAURENCE HOWARD LIEF
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/8/26. (No earlier availability.) A copy of the lost will still has not been filed. Proof of Holographic Instrument does not appear to have been filed. Deadline to provide courtesy copies of any new filed papers to the assigned examiner is 5/26/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GRACE LAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine to admit the decedent's will dated 12/7/1981 and appoint Karen Park Lee as Administrator with will annexed at a bond of $2,600,000. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
